Purpose: Create Agora-style module cover adapted to DEC/CSO Learning Hub.
Prompt / Production Direction: Create a premium e-learning module cover for 'Applying HRBA in CSO Practice — Module 1: Starting Your HRBA Learning Journey'. Left area should support deep navy text panel and Start button; right area should show a respectful Ethiopian/East African local CSO learning context such as staff reviewing a project plan or participating in a training. No readable text inside image.
Style + Brand: Professional, calm, realistic or semi-realistic; DEC colors #3B99D4, #91C852, #0F172A; clean and premium.
Safety restrictions: No real vulnerable people; no crisis/trauma imagery; no political symbols; no identifying documents.
Alt text: Illustration/photo-style visual of local CSO learners preparing to start an HRBA course module.

Purpose: Support the welcome video or transcript-first alternative.
Prompt / Production Direction: Create a clean welcome-video placeholder frame for the HRBA course, with a CSO facilitator/instructor style image and simple play area. Use neutral learning/training setting and DEC brand accents. Do not include readable text except short labels if necessary.
Style + Brand: Video-ready frame; white card on light background; high contrast.
Safety restrictions: No real organization logos except approved DEC/CSO Learning Hub; no sensitive community imagery.
Alt text: Welcome video frame showing a facilitator-style learning environment for HRBA course orientation.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create a clean horizontal/vertical roadmap graphic showing a learner journey from safe start and HRBA foundations through everyday rights, actor roles, principles, project design, implementation, MEAL, action planning, and completion. Use icons and simple cards, no long text.
Style + Brand: Flat vector infographic, DEC colors, accessible contrast.
Safety restrictions: No overcrowded text; provide text alternative in course.
Alt text: Roadmap showing the HRBA course journey from safe start to practical application and action planning.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create a simple illustration of a private learning portfolio or notebook with shield/check icon, suggesting safe personal learning notes and practical outputs. Use DEC blue/green, white cards, subtle shadows.
Safety restrictions: Do not show real names, documents, case records, or confidential files.
Alt text: Illustration of a private HRBA learning portfolio with safe learning symbols.

Purpose: Introduce Ayele as safe demo learner.
Prompt / Production Direction: Create a respectful semi-realistic avatar illustration of Ayele, a local CSO project officer using a private learning portfolio. Show professional, calm context: laptop/notebook, project planning notes without readable sensitive text. Avoid stereotypes.
Style + Brand: Semi-realistic avatar; warm, professional; DEC accents.
Safety restrictions: Fictional persona only; no real NGO logo; no sensitive data visible.
Alt text: Ayele, a local CSO project officer, using a private learning portfolio safely.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create an iconic graphic combining shield, notebook, anonymized data dots, and consent/check symbols to show safe learning, privacy, and no sensitive data sharing. Use clean white card and DEC blue/green.
Safety restrictions: No readable personal data, no real case imagery.
Alt text: Shield and notebook symbol showing safe, private HRBA learning.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create five consistent line/filled icons for dignity, participation, inclusion/non-discrimination, accountability, and sustainability/adaptation. Use DEC brand colors and simple labels if needed.
Style + Brand: Consistent icon set; white cards; accessible contrast.
Safety restrictions: No stereotypes; avoid complex symbols that imply political affiliation.
Alt text: Icons representing dignity, participation, inclusion, accountability, and sustainability.

Purpose: Show shift from beneficiary to rights-holder.
Prompt / Production Direction: Create a clear side-by-side illustration showing a shift from people as passive recipients to people as rights-holders with voice, agency, and accountability channels. Use respectful local CSO/community setting.
Safety restrictions: Do not depict communities as helpless; avoid savior narrative.
Alt text: Visual comparison showing a shift from passive beneficiary framing to rights-holder agency and voice.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create a scenario card illustration of a CSO team reviewing a project plan and asking better HRBA questions before acting. Include visual cues of participation, barriers, roles, and accountability, but no readable sensitive documents.
Safety restrictions: No real names, logos, complaints, political or safeguarding details.
Alt text: Fictional CSO team discussing rights-based planning questions before taking action.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create four simple icons for charity-based, needs-based, service-delivery, and rights-based practice framing. Icons should be distinct and neutral, avoiding judgmental or stigmatizing imagery.
Style + Brand: Flat line icons; DEC palette with neutral grey support.
Safety restrictions: No negative stereotypes; rights-based icon should suggest voice/accountability, not legal threat.
Alt text: Icons for charity-based, needs-based, service-delivery, and rights-based practice framing.

Purpose: Show actor relationship and CSO support role.
Prompt / Production Direction: Create a clean node-link diagram showing rights-holders, duty-bearers, CSO support/accountability role, and stakeholders/influencers. Use arrows for claims, responsibilities, participation, evidence, dialogue, and accountability. Keep labels short.
Style + Brand: Diagram/infographic; white card; DEC blue/green; accessible contrast.
Safety restrictions: No real actor names; use generic categories only.
Alt text: Diagram showing rights-holders, duty-bearers, CSO support role, and accountability relationships.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create an accessible horizontal response scale from 0 to 3 plus N/A: Not yet in place, Early steps, Partly in place, Consistently in place, Not applicable/need to check. Use clear labels and icons.
Safety restrictions: Do not imply score is public or certificate-related.
Alt text: Response scale for a private HRBA starting-point self-assessment.

Prompt / Production Direction: Create a professional wrap-up illustration showing a learner completing Module 1 and preparing to continue to Module 2, with a checklist/roadmap and calm progress indicator. Avoid childish celebration.
Safety restrictions: No trophy/exam pressure; no real data visible.
Alt text: Learner completing Module 1 and preparing to continue to Module 2.
